blurb: Mother's Day is May 10, and Roswell is making the whole weekend count with a garden tour just across the county line. It's a self-paced, outdoor event — the kind that feels like a legitimate outing without requiring a full-day commitment. Great for treating a mom who appreciates something a little more personal than brunch. Great for being the mom who actually wants to go somewhere beautiful on her own weekend. Pair it with lunch in historic Roswell and you've got a whole afternoon.
scoring_notes: The Mother's Day timing gives this real urgency and emotional relevance for an audience that skews 42, homeowning, and established — many of whom are either moms or buying experiences for moms. Garden tours appeal strongly to this demographic's lifestyle and income level. Docked on uniqueness because the details in the candidate listing are thin (community post, not a major organized event), and it may be smaller-scale than it sounds. Included as third pick because the timing hook is strong and it fills a 'what to do for Mother's Day' need the other two events don't address.

event_name: Mean Girls (The Musical) – City Springs Theatre Company
venue: Byers Theatre at City Springs, Sandy Springs
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Mean Girls (The Musical) – City Springs Theatre Company**
📅 Opens Friday, May 1 | Byers Theatre at City Springs, Sandy Springs
🎟️ Tickets vary by seat | [Get Tickets](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/27/mean-girls-comes-to-sandy-springs-may-1/)

You already know the movie. You probably know the lines. But City Springs Theatre Company is bringing Mean Girls to the Byers stage as a full-blown musical — and it plays completely differently when it's live in front of you.

This is not a touring production passing through. This is your local theatre company putting it on right here in Sandy Springs. The writing is sharp, the songs are genuinely catchy, and it's the kind of show that works whether you're going with a girlfriend group, a date, or honestly just yourself on a weeknight.

It opens May 1, which means you can catch it before the whole neighborhood is talking about it. Grab your tickets at cityspringstheatre.com before the good seats go.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for an adult-skewing, educated, $105K median income audience in Sandy Springs. Mean Girls as a musical has broad appeal across the 30s–50s demographic — the movie is nostalgic for some and current-culture for others. It's a local production at a neighborhood venue, which adds a community angle. Not a one-night-only event, but it's a new production opening this week, which gives it timeliness. Family-skew is low (not really a kids show), which suits this more adult-leaning audience well.

time: Fri 4–10:30 PM | Sat 11 AM–10:30 PM | Sun 11 AM–4 PM
venue: St. Nicholas Greek Orthodox Church, Marietta
blurb: The Marietta Greek Festival is back for a full three-day run, and it's the kind of event that earns its own weekend on the calendar. Think lamb on the spit, loukoumades, live Greek music, traditional dancing, and a church courtyard that turns into one of the best outdoor parties in Cobb County all spring. It's not a chain restaurant theme night — it's the real thing, put on by a tight-knit community that's been doing this for decades. Friday night has that date-night energy. Saturday is peak everything. Sunday is perfect if you want it a little more relaxed. Tickets and hours are at mariettagreekfestival.com — go ahead and put all three days in your phone.
scoring_notes: Annual but genuinely anticipated — this is the kind of community event East Cobb residents mark on the calendar. Strong fit for the 40s-and-up crowd, both couples and families with older kids. Accessible price point, easy to make a night out of it. Happening this week (May 15–17), well within the newsletter's publication window.

event_name: Dirt Roads and Footpaths — Coro Vocati Concert
venue: St. Catherine's Episcopal Church, East Cobb
blurb: If you've never heard of Coro Vocati, here's your introduction: they're an Atlanta chamber choral ensemble, and this Sunday they're performing right here in East Cobb at St. Catherine's Episcopal Church. The concert runs from 3 to 5 p.m. and it's called 'Dirt Roads and Footpaths' — which already tells you this isn't a stiff, formal affair. It's an afternoon of live choral music in a beautiful church space, the kind of thing that feels like a genuine discovery rather than something you found on a poster. Free up your Sunday afternoon and go.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for the educated, culturally engaged segment of the East Cobb audience — especially empty nesters looking for something other than a bar or a festival. Choral chamber music is niche but exactly right for a $118K median income, 65% college-educated neighborhood. One-time performance adds urgency. Happening this Sunday (May 3), so timing is tight but workable for a newsletter publishing this week.

event_name: Springfest at Round Trip Brewing Co.
venue: Round Trip Brewing Co., East Cobb
blurb: Round Trip Brewing is throwing a Springfest and it sounds exactly like a good Saturday. German-inspired beers, food, craft activities, patio vibes, and live music in the evening — all of it right here in East Cobb. It's casual enough to show up with no plan, but there's enough going on that you'll stay longer than you expected. Grab someone worth sitting outside with, pick up a beer, and let the evening do the rest. No ticket required.
scoring_notes: Great fit for the adult and empty-nester segment of this audience — low barrier to entry, local, casual but fun. German beer and patio music on a May evening plays very well with a 42-median-age crowd. Uniqueness is the weakest score here (brewery events happen regularly), but the German theme and live music give it more character than a typical tap-room night. East Cobb location is a strong plus.
